authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR 543.18 What are the minimum internal control standards for the cage, vault, kiosk, cash and cash equivalents?
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal regulation is the primary public baseline for tribal gaming cage, vault, kiosk, patron-deposit, variance, and segregation-of-duties controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Lane-wide cage control framing and prerequisite review
used_for: kiosk, patron-deposit, and cash-equivalent control framing

authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR 543.18 Cage, Vault, Kiosk, Cash and Cash Equivalent Toolkit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NIGC toolkit is an official regulator-issued workflow reference that translates 25 CFR 543.18 into concrete procedures, documentation checkpoints, and variance handling expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cage execution, documentation, and variance handling

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards: Cage and Credit
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada's cage and credit MICS are authoritative public operating standards for cashier accountability, chip inventories, patron deposits, wagering-account reports, reconciliations, and cage variance handling in a major gaming jurisdiction.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cash, chip, voucher, and patron-account transaction handling

authoritative_sources.title: CPA MICS Compliance Checklists and Guidelines - Cage and Credit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official NGCB checklist is a direct public workflow and evidence reference for cage accountability, documentation retention, reconciliations, and audit follow-up.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Exception investigation and closeout evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69D Casino Rules
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New Jersey's casino rules are an authoritative public source for cashiering controls, patron check handling, slot booth and cashier-window records, and internal-control obligations in another major gaming jurisdiction.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cashiering, check-cashing, and patron-funds control handling

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69E Rules of the Games
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New Jersey's Chapter 69E is an authoritative public rule source for chip, plaque, marker, cash-equivalent, and cashiers' cage handling constraints that materially shape cage workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Chip, plaque, marker, and cash-equivalent transaction handling

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions Casino Recordkeeping, Reporting, and Compliance Program Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FinCEN is the primary U.S. federal AML authority for casinos and this official FAQ is authoritative for front-money and wagering-account recordkeeping, multiple transaction logs, suspicious transaction escalation, and retention boundaries relevant to cage work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cash, account, and BSA-sensitive prerequisite review

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance on Preparing a Complete and Sufficient Suspicious Activity Report Narrative
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FinCEN's SAR narrative guidance is the authoritative public workflow reference for suspicious-activity evidence preservation and for distinguishing cage packaging from formal AML or SAR ownership.

authoritative_sources.title: GLI-16 Cashless Systems and Technologies
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GLI-16 is the recognized vendor-neutral public standard used across gaming jurisdictions for cashless transfers, patron accounts, kiosks, redemption trails, and audit logging that intersect cage execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cashless transfer, kiosk, and account audit-trace handling

workflow_stages.name: Cage intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the transaction, shift, patron or bank context, jurisdiction, delegated authority, and governing control set before cage work progresses.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cash, chip, voucher, and account transaction handling
workflow_stages.description: Use cage slips, inventories, patron-account records, and kiosk or voucher evidence to decide whether the cage item can advance, hold, rework, or route elsewhere.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and variance management
workflow_stages.description: Use cage accountability, vault counts, account balances, and reconciliation outputs to track unresolved shortages, overages, unsupported corrections, and rework states.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion and fulfillment evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Require cage-specific proof of transaction posting, redemption, receipt, balancing, or inventory disposition before the item can be completed.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: AML, credit, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when suspicious activity, licensing or credit boundaries, regulator-sensitive conflicts, or policy-control conflicts materially change whether cage work can continue.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Cage accountability forms, main bank inventories, vault inventories, cashier imprest records, and shift reconciliation worksheets
Fill slips, credit slips, chip and token inventories, jackpot funding support, and inventory movement records
Voucher issuance, redemption, kiosk settlement, and exception reports
Patron deposit, front-money, and wagering-account transfer, withdrawal, and balance records
Multiple transaction logs, CTR support, suspicious-activity indicators, and surveillance references
Approved internal controls, TICS or SICS where applicable, delegated authority matrices, and follow-up packets
systems_of_record: cage management or casino management system
vault, chip-bank, and inventory accountability records
front-money, patron deposit, and wagering-account platforms
surveillance, AML, and compliance case systems
document repositories for approved internal controls and delegated approvals

allowed: Assess whether documented cage records support advancing, holding, reworking, or completing a cage queue item.
Prepare evidence-backed execution updates, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages tied to slips, inventories, account records, and balancing evidence.
Request missing prerequisites and record-of-truth evidence before changing lifecycle state.
escalate: Suspicious cash, patron-account, or transaction patterns are present.
Licensing, credit, compliance, accounting, or regulator-facing authority is needed to resolve the item.
Public guidance, approved internal controls, and tenant SOPs conflict materially on cage, kiosk, or account handling.
Jurisdiction, property type, or control-set differences change the evidence required and the tenant mapping is unavailable.
refuse: Requests to alter cage, voucher, kiosk, inventory, or patron-account facts without authoritative source evidence
Requests to approve new credit, write-offs, policy overrides, licensing outcomes, or formal AML filings
Requests that treat cage work as generic cashiering or customer service without cage-specific records

domain_failure_modes: Completing or correcting a cage transaction without authoritative slips, receipts, inventories, or account evidence
Treating suspicious or structured cash activity as ordinary player-service work in order to close a queue item quickly
Closing a vault, kiosk, voucher, or patron-account variance without cage-specific reconciliation and source-of-truth validation
Ignoring jurisdiction-specific cage controls, approved internal controls, or segregation-of-duties requirements because the request sits in an operations queue
Flattening cage work into generic cashiering language that omits fills, credits, banks, chips, vouchers, kiosks, patron deposits, and wagering-account records

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific differences in cage structure, kiosk controls, and patron-account pathways
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tribal, Nevada, and New Jersey baselines differ materially on cage organization, kiosk treatment, chip handling, and patron-account movement, which can change required evidence and approvals.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of governing jurisdiction, approved internal controls, and tenant cage topology before final progression, completion, or variance resolution.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific delegation for cage supervisors, variance thresholds, and player-funds approvals
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baselines define control expectations, but tenant governance decides which refunds, corrections, transfers, or exceptions cage operations may execute directly.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve the tenant authority matrix and keep outputs recommendation-only whenever approval ownership or variance authority is unresolved.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because the remaining ambiguity is explicitly constrained to jurisdiction and tenant retrieval at runtime.
The research output constrains the lane to cage execution-state management with explicit cash, account, BSA-sensitive, and approval-boundary discipline.
